ideal_must_detect.matched_text: The incident did not meet PDPL breach thresholds; this is a compliance memo documenting controls, not a breach notice, and DPA Schedule 4 (breach notification) was not triggered.

On 04–05 June, a misconfigured SFTP path during vendor onboarding triggered a halt on exporting client onboarding forms to Riyada Data Services Ltd (Bahrain) pending data-export approval.
The forms contain PDPL personal data (name, contact, masked Emirates ID token EID-XX4321, family-origin disclosure for KYC) and a trade licence copy for corporate signatories (TL-90877-AL), covered by DPA Schedule 2.
Cross-border transfer was not executed until the Transfer Impact Assessment and Data-Export Approval Memo DEAM-1194 were signed; only test hashes left the UAE during the pause
ideal_must_detect.reason: moved from must_detect after strict runtime no longer emitted exact span during Stage B baseline reconciliation

must_detect.matched_text: The incident did not meet PDPL breach thresholds; this is a compliance memo documenting controls, not a breach notice, and DPA Schedule 4 (breach notification) was not triggered.
The incident did not meet PDPL breach thresholds; this is a compliance memo documenting controls, not a breach notice, and DPA Schedule 4 (breach notification) was not triggered.
The incident did not meet PDPL breach thresholds; this is a compliance memo documenting controls, not a breach notice, and DPA Schedule 4 (breach notification) was not triggered.

We received a DSAR from Mr. Zayed Al Qamar requesting access to processing records; fulfillment was completed within 10 working days with extraction logs attached.
Ms. Laila Al Mansouri withdrew consent for marketing emails on 03 June; suppression list updated and confirmation sent, with erasure of marketing attributes completed at 14:22 GST.
No Emirates ID images or trade licence scans left the UAE; the data lake applied minimisation by dropping family-origin fields not required for AML/CTF, per Retention Schedule RS-9/UA.
Deal workflow context: the ops queue briefly linked to “Project Saffron Falcon” (issuer onboarding) but audit shows no access to non-public issuer decks; information barriers and wall-cross logs remained intact.
In line with UAE financial free zone practices, our internal market-abuse follow-up found no MNPI exposure and no obligation to notify the Gulf Securities Authority or Pearl Exchange.
The incident did not meet PDPL breach thresholds; this is a compliance memo documenting controls, not a breach notice, and DPA Schedule 4 (breach notification) was not triggered.
must_detect.reason: promoted from strict runtime finding during Stage B baseline reconciliation
